hard.next_step: Meeting with Pölki and the CPO (referenced as Herr Flandorfer) on 1 Jul to lock commercial flexibility and the 7-year S2C terms; return with reworked agentic-AI pricing volumes.
hard.ais_why: Product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake and AppXtend across S2P + Intake with extensive S2P modules; opp name embeds 'Intake' (Merlin Intake AI). Engaged BD, Shortlisted, EY-advised RFP, dual AI products.
hard.competitor: Coupa;GEP;WPS / Allocation Network (incumbent P2P);Orchestration overlay (bolt-on);Do-nothing / deferral

north_star_verdict.headline: Functionally preferred after a won POC and a strong 1 Jul onsite where commercials converged on a 7-year S2C core with procurement carved out to 2028/2029, but the 23 Jul close is not credible: the buyer named two start scenarios (Q4 2026 or mid-2027) and IT/SAP resources stay blocked by the S/4HANA migration and Turkey rollout.
north_star_verdict.math: Days to close ~17 (close 2026-07-23, today 2026-07-06). Shortlisted since 2026-04-30, ~67 days in stage. Onsite commercial alignment held 1 Jul; pricing converging (7-year S2C, price guaranteed 2 years, procurement/P2P optional from 2028/2029, amount cut 650k->448,680 on 25 Jun). Remaining before signature: CPO/management sign-off, IT/CIO SAP-resource commitment, EY implementation scoping, MSA/Order Form redlines (paper shared 18 May, not yet redlined). Buyer said on 1 Jul it could not commit an exact timeline and 'jede Woche zählt' to sign S2C this year; realistic signature is Q4 2026, not 23 Jul. Deal is healthy and engaged; the sole real issue is the date.
evidence: 1 Jul onsite (Teil 1 and Teil 2): the group procurement lead joined the commercial call; both sides agreed to keep S2C core as-is, carve out the procurement/P2P module as optional (start 2028/2029), and hold the price with reworked agentic-AI volumes (Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026).
Buyer intent to sign S2C this year: 'eigentlich hätten wir jetzt schon zu schreiben wollen ... jede Woche zählt mittlerweile' (Pölki, Zycus Onsite Teil 1, 1 Jul 2026).
Buyer could not commit a firm timeline: two possible starts (S2C this year or mid-2027), procurement/P2P deferred to 2028 (the group procurement lead, Zycus Onsite Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026).
IT resource gate persists: signing S2C this year still needs the CIO to free SAP resources blocked by the S/4HANA migration and the Turkey SAP rollout (Zycus Onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026).
Buyer requested a management meeting with the CPO (referenced as Herr Flandorfer) to lock commercial flexibility and terms (Pricing Structure call, 25 Jun 2026; reconfirmed 1 Jul).
POC completed 6-13 May with positive functional and AI feedback (POC feedback / Zycus Feedback Testing call, 11 Jun 2026).
Amount reduced 650,000 to 448,680 on 25 Jun reflecting the procurement carve-out; within the buyer's stated 400-500K SaaS budget (SF field history 25 Jun; budget stated 24 Mar per Next_Step).
Upside Key Deal on a 23 Jul close is not defensible: no CPO/management sign-off, no IT resource commitment, no contract redlines started. The deal is genuinely Best Case on a corrected Q4 2026 close.

metrics.narrative: The value story is qualitative, not quantified. The group procurement lead was explicit on 1 Jul that Austrian Post is already highly digitalized so 'there is no real sense of urgency to change everything', and that the internal business cases 'are fighting against each other' for budget, so procurement must prove value to rank the investment. Group spend is about EUR 1 billion across roughly 35 procurement staff, and the upside is AI/agentic automation plus a unified S2P suite replacing the aging WPS landscape and the separate procurement information system, but no ROI, savings, or cycle-time numbers are on the table. Quantifying the business case is the single highest-value MEDDPICC action.

economic_buyer.narrative: The commercial/pricing authority is visible but Zycus has not yet engaged it directly. On 1 Jul the group procurement lead (head of group procurement at Austrian Post Group) ran the commercial conversation, framing it as the 'commercial stage' that 'now needs really management attention and commitment from both sides'. The ultimate budget approver is the CPO, referenced in the deal next steps as Herr Flandorfer, whom the rep on 25 Jun asked to meet at management level to lock flexibility ('wenn wir auf Management Ebene noch mal sprechen können oder mit Ihnen und Flandorfer'). Neither the group procurement lead nor the CPO is a mapped Salesforce contact, and Zycus has not met the CPO. Booking that management meeting is the move that converts converged commercials into a signature.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are well documented across use-case reviews and the POC. They are: (1) SAP / WPS4 integration feasibility, tested by Markus Dernitzky against WPS open APIs, with a worst-case Intake-link bridge to the incumbent catalog if deeper integration is not achievable (Teil 1, 1 Jul); (2) genuine agentic-AI depth, where Amit drew the point-agent vs orchestrated-agent distinction and the buyer team responded by increasing agentic volumes 50-70% (Teil 2, 1 Jul); (3) one integrated S2P suite that replaces WPS and the separate procurement information system via Intake as the front door; (4) commercial flexibility and a phased rollout; and (5) total cost transparency versus current systems, which the buyer asked to see as a scenario comparison. Zycus leads on function and AI per the 11 Jun POC feedback.
sources: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026
S2C / P2P Use Case reviews, 26-30 Mar 2026

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, EY-advised RFP through the ÖPAG supplier portal: the commercial RFP round was published 18 Mar, Zycus was shortlisted to the top 3-4, ran a POC 6-13 May, and held an onsite commercial alignment on 1 Jul. The approval chain reads from the call sequence: the project team (Pölki as strategic buyer and co-lead, Karin Eppich on the system side, Melissa Koller for P2P) drives it up to management and the CPO (Flandorfer), with the board setting overall investment priority. EY leads implementation scoping and IT/CIO must release SAP resources. The presence of the group procurement lead and Zycus's Amit on 1 Jul signals the deal has escalated to the commercial-decision level, with standing weekly 'Blocker' meetings keeping cadence.
sources: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026
SF Next_Step__c engagement timeline (RFP 18 Mar, shortlist 21 Apr)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partly in motion but no redlines have started. Zycus shared its MSA (Rahmenvertrag) plus a standard Order Form with SLAs on 18 May at Pölki's request. The 1 Jul offer is license-only; implementation and the SOW will be scoped with partner EY once IT green-lights the integration effort. Commercially the buyer is discussing a 7-year S2C term with the price guaranteed for two years and the procurement/P2P module optional from 2028/2029; on 25 Jun the buyer even floated a six or seven-year term to hold pricing. As an Austrian buyer no PO gate has been raised, so paper visibility now depends on the CPO sign-off and starting MSA/Order Form redlines, neither of which has begun.

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is an aging, fragmented procurement landscape: the incumbent WPS / Allocation Network P2P (acquired, weak post-acquisition support) sits on an older WPS4 build, and the buying team was surprised how far behind it is on SAP integration. The buyer wants one interconnected S2P suite with AI and a partner that brings use-case ideas, and wants to retire its separate procurement information system, which Zycus S2C can replace. The pain owners are Pölki and Eppich on the systems side and Koller for P2P. The countervailing reality, stated by the group procurement lead on 1 Jul, is that high existing digitalization means urgency is low, so the pain must be converted into a ranked, quantified business case to unlock budget.
sources: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026

champion.narrative: There is a strong internal champion network. Pölki, the strategic buyer and project co-lead, described the current commercial move as 'unser Strohhalm von der Melissa, von der Karin und von mir, dass wir das Ding hier einfangen können' (25 Jun), i.e. he, Karin Eppich and Melissa Koller are actively trying to lock Zycus in. Eppich (system-side project lead) stated a preference for Zycus on 16 Mar and drives the technical workstream; she also offered to get the rep in front of the CPO ('Beim Herrn Flandorfer bringe ich irgendwas hin', 25 Jun). What is still missing is not advocacy but air cover: the champions cannot commit budget or IT resources, so they need Zycus to arm them with a quantified ROI and an IT-resource story to carry upward.

competition.narrative: This is a live head-to-head with Coupa: on 1 Jul the buyer confirmed running similar SAP-API integration conversations with Coupa ('ich hör da raus, dass Sie da ähnliches Gespräch auch schon mit den Kollegen von Coupa hatten'), and the rep noted Coupa turns very flexible in a head-to-head. Zycus leads on function and AI per the 11 Jun POC and is inside budget (448,680 vs Coupa's ~475K SaaS), so the wedge is commercial flexibility plus the integrated agentic-AI edge, not features. GEP is on the RFP shortlist but invisible on calls, and an orchestration-overlay bolt-on tempts IT as a low-effort path. The single strongest current threat is not a rival vendor but do-nothing / deferral, driven by the IT SAP-resource gate and an internal budget fight, sharpened by the board putting its biggest logistics project on hold on 24 Jun.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is real and forward: a won POC in May, positive feedback 11 Jun, and an onsite commercial alignment on 1 Jul that converged pricing on a 7-year S2C core with procurement carved out. The single open issue is the signature date, hostage to the IT SAP-resource gate and internal budget ranking.

items.change: Onsite commercial alignment: group procurement lead joined; converged on 7-year S2C core, procurement/P2P optional from 2028/2029, price held with reworked agentic-AI volumes.
Amount reduced 650,000 to 448,680 reflecting the procurement carve-out; buyer requested a management meeting with the CPO.
Close date pushed 30 Jun to 23 Jul and forecast moved Best Case to Upside Key Deal; AI-based SAP/S4HANA integration proposal emailed to Pölki (~70% internal-effort reduction).
POC feedback confirmed positive on function and AI (Zycus Feedback Testing).
POC executed onsite (S2C and P2P use cases) 6-13 May with positive results.

competitive_position.summary: Ranked by current threat: do-nothing / deferral is the biggest threat (IT SAP resources blocked by S/4HANA and Turkey, plus an internal budget fight after the board put its largest logistics project on hold, 24 Jun-1 Jul). Coupa is the live external head-to-head, running parallel SAP-API integration talks and matching on commercial flexibility, though Zycus leads on function and AI after the 11 Jun POC and is inside budget. An orchestration-overlay bolt-on tempts IT as the low-effort path. GEP remains a shortlisted but invisible name. Incumbent WPS / Allocation Network is being displaced and is dormant as a threat.

competitors.quote: zwei mögliche Timelines: start S2C this year or mid-2027 ... jede Woche zählt; and the biggest logistics project was just put on hold by the board
competitors.source: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026; Pricing Structure call 25 Jun 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Remove IT's rationale with the AI SAP-integration proof (cut internal effort ~70%) and arm procurement with a quantified ROI to win the internal budget ranking.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: ich hör da raus, dass Sie da ähnliches Gespräch auch schon mit den Kollegen von Coupa hatten (buyer confirms parallel API talks with Coupa)
competitors.source: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1, 1 Jul 2026; Pricing Structure call 25 Jun 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Functionally preferred and within budget (448,680 vs Coupa's ~475K); counter Coupa's commercial flexibility with the multi-year price-lock and the integrated agentic-AI edge, backed by the POC result.

competitors.name: Orchestration overlay (bolt-on to existing P2P)
competitors.quote: other players offer orchestration tools as an overlay on the existing P2P to avoid SAP integration; attractive to IT but weak on reporting, platform benefits and transformation
competitors.how_we_win: Prove Zycus SAP integration is low-effort via AI iSaaS and show the overlay's reporting/AI gaps; offer Zycus Intake bridged to the current P2P as the interim.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: GEP is in the RFP shortlist (top 3-4) but has not surfaced on any buyer call or POC discussion
competitors.how_we_win: Maintain the functional and AI differentiation and lock IT commitment before GEP can re-engage.

competitors.name: WPS / Allocation Network (incumbent P2P)
competitors.quote: aging WPS build (WPS4), poor post-acquisition support; buyer wants to replace it
competitors.source: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1, 1 Jul 2026; system demo 19 May 2025
competitors.how_we_win: Baseline to beat, not a competitor; demonstrate full-suite benefits and Intake replacing the WPS front door.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is aligned on the criteria that matter most (agentic-AI depth, one integrated suite, commercial flexibility and phasing) and partially aligned on SAP/WPS4 integration pending an IT verdict; the one exposed area is total cost transparency, where the buyer asked for a scenario comparison not yet delivered.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Functionally preferred after a won May POC and converged commercials on 1 Jul (7-year S2C, price held, procurement carved out to 2028); the only thing between us and the win is a firm signature date, hostage to blocked IT/SAP resources and an internal budget fight.

win_position.text: Shortlisted caps confidence near 70, and this earns it: a won POC (positive function + AI feedback, 11 Jun), buyer preference (Eppich stated a preference 16 Mar; the team 'favors Zycus'), and in-budget pricing (448,680 vs Coupa's ~475K).
Buyer leaning our way: Pölki called the current commercial move 'unser Strohhalm ... dass wir das Ding hier einfangen können' (25 Jun) and both sides converged on a 7-year S2C on 1 Jul.
Coupa is a live head-to-head running parallel SAP-API integration talks and matches Zycus on commercial flexibility (Pölki, 1 Jul; rep, 25 Jun).

Scope narrowed (650k to 448,680, procurement deferred to 2028/2029) and no quantified ROI yet, so procurement risks losing the internal budget ranking after the board paused its biggest logistics project (1 Jul).

deal_momentum.text: The 1 Jul onsite brought the group procurement lead into the room and converged pricing on a 7-year S2C with the price held (Onsite Teil 1/2, 1 Jul).
Buyer pull is strong: standing weekly Blocker meetings and Pölki saying 'jede Woche zählt' with intent to sign S2C this year (1 Jul).
Buyer could not commit a firm timeline and named two scenarios (Q4 2026 or mid-2027), so the 23 Jul close will slip (1 Jul).

customer_commitment.text: Buyer completed a full POC (6-13 May), requested a management meeting with CPO Flandorfer, and is discussing a 7-year term with price guaranteed two years (25 Jun; 1 Jul).
Commitment is capped by the IT/CIO resource gate (SAP resources blocked by S/4HANA and Turkey) and no contract redlines have started (1 Jul).

deal_risk.text: The 23 Jul close is not credible; realistic signature is Q4 2026 pending CPO sign-off and IT resource release.
Internal budget fight after the board paused the biggest logistics project; procurement must rank the investment with an ROI it does not yet have (1 Jul).
Coupa remains a live parallel evaluation on both integration and commercials.

timeline.evidence: Buyer could not commit a date; 23 Jul not credible; Q4 2026 or 2027 scenarios (1 Jul).

scope.evidence: Scope reduced 650k to 448,680; procurement/P2P deferred to 2028/2029.

Defensive/phased rather than a loss signal, but it lowers this-year value and reflects the internal budget pressure.
